rulings.Case Title: DEVIN STEELE et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Ruling On Motion To Compel And Re-Set Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 10, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFFS DEVIN STEELE AND LILIA STEELE'S Motion To Vacate Ruling On Motion To Compel And Re-Set Motion. DENIED. THE MANDATORY PROVISIONS OF CCP SECTION 473 DO NOT APPLY. SEE ENGLISH V. IKON BUSINESS SOLUTIONS (2001) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 130. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. NO PROPOSED PLEADING IS ATTACHED TO THE MOTION. CCP SECTION 473(b). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DEBRA VALDEZ VS. BANK OF AMERICA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 10, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERCIA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN AND PLAINTIFF RAISES NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION (BREACH OF EXPRESS CONTRACT FOR SEVERANCE AND BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT RE SEVERANCE) AS CONCEDED. THE NATIONAL BANK ACT ("NBA") APPLIES AND PRE-EMPTS THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS AN OFFICER PURSUANT TO THE NBA AND DESCRIBED HER AUTHORITY AT DEPOSITION. (PAGES 87-95; 134-135, 161-162 AND 186.) PLAINTIFF WOULD ACT AS BRANCH MANAGER WHEN THE MANAGER AND ASSISTANT MANAGER WERE ABSENT (PG 89:16-24), COULD SIGN CASHIERS CHECKS UP TO $100,000 (PG. 90), COULD APPROVE HOLD ON DEPOSIT OVERRIDES (PG. 91) AND HAD AUTHORITY TO WAIVE OVERDRAFTS (PG. 92). SHE OFTEN OPENED THE BANK (PG. 134) FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT DEMONSTRATES GOOD CAUSE TO DISCHARGE PLAINTIFF. UMF'S 16-20 SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S BALANCE ACCURACY WAS BELOW THE REQUIRED 88% AND SHE WAS WRITTEN UP SEVERAL TIMES. AT HER DEPOSITION, PAGE 257, PLAINTIFF ADMITS SHE WAS NOT MEETING THE GOAL AND CHENDRA WAS NOT HAPPY WITH HER PERFORMANCE. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SHOW PRETEXTUAL OR BAD FAITH DISCHARGE. THE STATEMENT THAT IS PURPORTEDLY DEFAMATION IS TRUE AND THE MANAGER'S PRIVILEGE APPLIES. CC SECTION 47(c). PLAINTIFF HAS NO EVIDENCE SHOWING MALICE. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 437c(h) IS DENIED, INSUFFICIENT SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN LUM ARCHITECTURE, INC VS. ARGYLE INVESTMENT PARTNERS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 10, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANT ASIA SF, LLC, GREGORY LOCKAMY, WILBUR YOUNG, and LARRY HASHBARGER DEMURRER TO first AMENDED COMPLAINT. The court vacates its ruling of October 22, 2010. The demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the first cause of action. Plaintiff must plead exhaustion of the mediation/arbitration remedies in the written contracts, or specific factual allegations showing waiver. See Carean & Co. vs. Security Pac Business Credit, Inc. (1990) 222 Cal. App. 3d 1371. Plaintiff must also plead which defendants are liable under each contract and what damages are due under each contract. Demurrer overruled as to second, third and fourth cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MEDLEY CAPITAL LLC VS. MILBANK, TWEED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Production Of Docs; Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 10, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT MILBANK, TWEED, HADLEY & MCCLOY LLP Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Production Of Docs. GRANTED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED AS TO REQUESTS 18-21, 29-30, AND 31-32. THE RESPONSES ARE LIMITED TO REAL ESTATE INVESTMENTS IN NEBRASKA BY PLAINTIFF AND ITS AFFILIATES. DENIED AS TO REQUESTS 27-28. GRANTED AS TO REQUESTS 25-26. SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAD SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR ITS POSITION. COURT STRIKES UNTIMELY SURREPLY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. ELIABETH LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 10, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT ELIABETH LEE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH ONE FINAL 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING TO BRING THESE CLAIMS. NONE OF THE ALLEGED CAUSES OF ACTION REQUIRE OWNERSHIP OF THE PROPERTY AS A PREREQUISITE TO BRINGING SUIT. AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MAY NOT ALLEGE STATEMENTS THAT TOOK PLACE BEFORE 8-11-09. THESE ARE RES JUDICATA OF CASE #478790. SEE ALPHA MECHANICAL V. TRAVELERS CASUALTY (2005) 133 CAL.APP.4TH 1319, 1330-31. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE STATEMENTS THAT CLEARLY OCCURRED AFTER 8-11-09. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD WHETHER HE IS CLAIMING INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS OR PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC RELATIONS AND FACTS, INCLUDING DATES, OF ALLEGED INTERFERENCES. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING NEGLIGENCE AND SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN KIGHTLINGER VS. MARION FELDMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Modify The Subpoenas Of Defts Marion Feldman And Philip Barry Feldman
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 10, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF STEPHEN KIGHTLINGER'S Motion To Modify The Subpoenas Of Defendants Marion Feldman And Philip Barry Feldman. DENIED. THE COURT CONSIDERS THIS MOTION TO BE A MOTION TO QUASH. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENT OF A SEPARATE STATEMENT, CRC 3.1345. ON THE MERITS, DEFENDANTS AGREE TO LIMIT THE MEDICAL SUBPOENAS TO THE BODY PARTS PLAINTIFF CLAIMS WERE INJURED IN THE ACCIDENT. THE COLLATERAL SOURCE RULE DOES NOT PROHIBIT DISCOVERY OF THE REQUESTED RECORDS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN ENG VS. ELIZABETH LEE, et al.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 10, 2010, line 13, Defendant's ELIZABETH LEE MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. Motion for attorney fees denied, motion for costs granted. SFAC section 37.9 (f) awards attorney fees to the prevailing party when a tenant institutes a civil proceeding for a landlord's wrongful attempt to recover possession of a rental unit in violation of sections 37.9 and/or 37.10. Here, no such lawsuit was filed and no finding has been made as to plaintiff's alleged wrongful conduct. Defendant cites no other grounds such as a lease provision, for an award of attorney fees. Costs are awarded in the amount of $667.65. =(302/CWW)
